Functions to start and stop Bluetooth LE scanning and handle scan reports.

# Purpose
This header file provides a narrow functionality related to Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE) scanning operations. It declares two functions, `bt_driver_start_le_scan` and `bt_driver_stop_le_scan`, which control the start and stop of a BLE scan, respectively. The `bt_driver_start_le_scan` function allows configuration of the scan with parameters such as `active_scan`, `use_white_list_filter`, `filter_dups`, `scan_interval_ms`, and `scan_window_ms`, and returns a boolean indicating success or failure. The `bt_driver_stop_le_scan` function also returns a boolean indicating the success of stopping the scan. Additionally, the file declares an external callback function, `bt_driver_cb_le_scan_handle_report`, which processes scan reports received during the BLE scan. This file is intended to be included in other C++ source files that require BLE scanning capabilities.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `inttypes.h`
- `comm/ble/gap_le_scan.h`
